title: vanilla surinamensis
intro: Vanilla surinamensis, also known as the wild vanilla or flat-leaved vanilla, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of vanilla surinamensis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's roots or leaves in hot water.
